However, ultimately it is your choice as to whether you wish to use our website. If you have any questions regarding our privacy policy, please contact us at hello@theartoforthodontics. com. --- Accessibility Statement ---
The Art of Orthodontics is committed to facilitating the accessibility and usability of its website, theartoforthodontics. com, for everyone.

When we receive any request to access, edit or delete personal identifiable information we shall first take reasonable steps to verify your identity before granting you access or otherwise taking any action. This is important to safeguard your information. Our privacy policy has been compiled so as to comply with the law of every country or legal jurisdiction in which we aim to do business. If you think it fails to satisfy the law of your jurisdiction, we would like to hear from you.

We will never (and will not allow any third party to) use the statistical analytics tool to track or to collect any Personally Identifiable Information (PII) of visitors to our site. Google will not associate your IP address with any other data held by Google. Neither we nor Google will link, or seek to link, an IP address with the identity of a computer user.

We will not associate any data gathered from this site with any Personally Identifiable Information from any source, unless you explicitly submit that information via a fill-in form on our website. We record information such as your geographical location, your Internet service provider and your IP address. We also record information about the software you are using to browse our website, such as the type of computer or device and the screen resolution.

We are committed to creating beautiful, healthy smiles. From your first visit, our team takes the time to understand your needs and make your comfort our priority. With advanced technology and a caring approach, we ensure your orthodontic journey is smooth and enjoyable. Thank you for trusting us to help transform your smile.

This information is recorded by Google Analytics as well as via certain 3rd party plugins installed on our website. We use this information in aggregate to assess the popularity of the webpages on our website and how we perform in providing content to you. If you wish us to remove personally identifiable information from our systems, you may contact us at hello@theartoforthodontics. com.

The Art of Orthodontics aims to comply with all applicable standards, including the World Wide Web Consortiums Web Content Accessibility Guidelines 2. 0 up to Level AA (WCAG 2. 0 AA). The Art of Orthodontics is proud of the efforts that we have completed and that are in-progress to ensure that our website is accessible to everyone. Should you experience any difficulty in accessing any part of this website , please feel free to call us at 469-393-0333 or email us at hello@theartoforthodontics.

This proactive approach helps ensure a healthier, more functional smile as they grow. Teens Teen orthodontic treatment focuses on correcting alignment, bite issues, and overall smile aesthetics during a time of significant growth.

We understand that all patients are unique, so we shape our patient relationships around individual needs. We offer a range of orthodontic solutions designed to accommodate different abilities, ensuring a positive experience every step of the way.

With a variety of options, including traditional braces and clear aligners, we create treatment plans tailored to each teen’s needs. Our goal is to help them feel confident in their smile as they enter adulthood. Adults Adult orthodontic treatment offers personalized solutions to correct alignment and bite issues, whether for cosmetic or functional reasons. With discreet options like clear aligners and braces designed for adults, we make it easier to achieve a beautiful, healthy smile at any age.

Suzzane Horani, DDS, MS Background Little did I know that my exposure to orthodontics as a patient in my hometown of Oklahoma City, Oklahoma would pique my interest and direct my future. I enjoy so many aspects of orthodontics as I continue to learn throughout the years.

Our approach is centered around the triad of oral health needs, facial aesthetics, and the advanced procedures and technology we use to ensure your best possible outcome. We take great pride in delivering transformative care by creating individualized treatment plans that balance functionality with aesthetics for children, teens, and adults. Every treatment is tailored to address your unique needs while enhancing your overall appearance and well-being.

Suite 130 Call Us Text Us --> 469-393-0333 Call Us Text Us About Course of Care Open menu Before After Process Treatments Braces Invisalign and Clear Aligners Orthognathic Surgery Technology Patient Info Open menu Current Patients New Patients Patient Forms Financing Insurance Referring Doctors Contact Schedule Consultation Our Course of Care Feel better, look better, be better At The Art of Orthodontics, we are dedicated to transforming lives through thoughtful, personalized care.

Prior to specializing in orthodontics, I practiced as a general dentist, only to reinforce the idea that the harmony of occlusion, healthy musculature, jaw position, and temporomandibular joint health lies in the expertise of comprehensive orthodontics. Orthodontics includes straightening teeth as well as setting an excellent foundation for successful lifelong oral health and overall general health.

My goal as an American Canadian Board-Certified Orthodontist is dedication to the delivery of excellent orthodontics that exceeds patients’ expectations and improves their overall quality of life. Approach Serendipity led me to meeting and learning from many orthodontic colleagues through mentorship and courses. This shaped my passion of artistically integrating foundational knowledge with the most up-to-date technology in orthodontics to deliver excellent care.

I wanted to gain experience as a general dentist prior to specialty training and completed a one-year advanced education general dentistry residency in Waco, Texas. After practicing general dentistry, I decided to pursue orthodontic training at the Eastman Institute for Oral Health University of Rochester Medical Center. I earned my Certificate in Orthodontics and Dentofacial Orthopedics as chief resident of my class.

I strive to adhere to the philosophy of delivering excellence in every aspect of our lives while also having fun. Credentials I attended The University of Texas at Austin and completed my Bachelor of Arts in Biology. I moved to San Antonio for dental school at the University Health Sciences Center in San Antonio. I attained my Doctor of Dental Surgery degree with distinctions in research and education.

Professional Memberships College of Diplomates, American Board of Orthodontics American Association of Orthodontists American Dental Association Southwestern Association of Orthodontists Texas Orthodontic Study Club Texas Dental Association Dallas County Dental Society Personal During my spare time, I enjoy many hobbies that trace back to my Oklahoma childhood days including golf, tennis, and…state fairs.

The Art of Orthodontics stands apart by combining cutting-edge techniques with compassionate care in a welcoming environment. By building lasting relationships and creating positive experiences, we ensure a welcoming environment where each patient receives the highest standard of care, leading to lasting improvements in both smile and self-esteem. Dr.

During this time, I also completed a Master of Science in Dental Sciences with a focus on clinical and translational sciences and was awarded as the recipient of the Northeastern Society of Orthodontics Resident Research Program.

These experiences have deepened our commitment to making a positive impact on global health and well-being. While we continue to serve communities abroad, we are eagerly expanding our local involvement. By supporting outreach programs and organizations in our area, we help strengthen the community we call home.

If I am not at the office, you may find me socializing with family, friends, or my handsome nephew. Welcome to the Space Where Care Takes Place Community Caring At Art of Orthodontics, we’re proud to give back to our communities. We believe that our community deserves a course of supportive care, and are proud to have participated in two dental mission trips, providing orthodontic care to underserved communities in Panama and Jordan.

If you have any additional questions, feel free to reach out to our team — we’re here to help. What should I expect during my first visit. Your first visit includes a comprehensive exam, digital images, and a discussion about your treatment options. Our team will ensure you feel comfortable and informed. How long does orthodontic treatment take. Treatment length varies by individual needs, typically lasting between 12 to 24 months. We’ll provide a personalized timeline for your specific case.

Do you accept insurance. Yes, we accept insurance as out-of-network and will work with your provider to maximize your savings. We also offer flexible financial options. What are clear aligners. Clear aligners are a discreet, removable alternative to traditional braces, gradually shifting teeth into place. They’re comfortable and nearly invisible. Can I pay for my treatment in installments.

Yes, we offer flexible payment plans, including third-party financing options, to help make treatment more affordable. Are there discounts available. We offer discounts for paying in full, as well as special pricing for families, friends, and referring doctors. Ask us about current promotions. Do you treat patients with special needs. Yes, we offer compassionate care tailored to the needs of patients with special needs, ensuring a comfortable experience. How do I schedule an appointment.

Intro to the Course of Care Our Assessment helps us determine your oral health needs and create a plan for achieving your best smile. Your Course of Care is our recommended treatment path that ensures optimal oral health and facial aesthetics. Our Technology is integrated into your Course of Care to assist in assessing your case and tracking your progress throughout treatment.

Consultation During your Consultation, we will take the time to answer all your questions and listen carefully to your thoughts about your treatment. This initial discussion is completely free of charge, and helps us understand your goals and ensures that we are aligned in creating a treatment plan tailored to your needs.

Before & After Process & Treatments Your Course of Care starts with a thorough two-stage Consultation and Assessment process designed to ensure the best possible outcome for your oral health and facial aesthetics. You may either complete both your Consultation and Assessment in one in-office visit, or have your Consultation via video session, followed by an in-office Assessment.

Assessment Your Assessment involves a detailed evaluation of your case, which includes photos and/or video of your face and teeth, X-rays, and scans of your teeth and soft tissue. This comprehensive review provides the data we need to create a precise treatment plan. Following your Assessment, Dr. Horani will provide an initial recommendation for your Course of Care, addressing both your oral health and enhancing your facial aesthetics.

By considering the relationship between your teeth and overall facial structure, we ensure your treatment complements your unique look. We treat patients of all ages, including those with special needs, providing individualized care tailored to their specific requirements. Our goal is to provide a result that not only improves dental function but also creates a smile that feels perfectly in sync with your face.

Suite 130 Call Us Text Us --> 469-393-0333 Call Us Text Us About Course of Care Open menu Before After Process Treatments Braces Invisalign and Clear Aligners Orthognathic Surgery Technology Patient Info Open menu Current Patients New Patients Patient Forms Financing Insurance Referring Doctors Contact Schedule Consultation The artful balance of Oral Health, Facial Aesthetics, and Course of Care Facially-Driven Orthodontics & Smile Design Facially-driven orthodontics focuses on aligning your smile with your natural facial features to achieve the best outcome for both oral health and facial harmony.

They are highly effective for correcting a variety of dental issues, from misalignment to bite problems. Twin brackets are a type of traditional braces with separate brackets for each tooth, providing precise control over tooth movement. Clear Clear braces offer a more discreet option with transparent brackets that blend in with your natural teeth. They provide the same effectiveness as traditional braces while being less noticeable.

You would not be able to find a better person for your treatment. Simone H. Dr. Horani is highly skilled, thorough, curious, thoughtful, compassionate, as well as incredibly kind. She is everything I look for in a health care provider. She is also an artist. I wish everyone could receive the care she and her support crew provides. They are exceptional. I am so glad I found her Laura L. I can’t say enough good things about Dr.

Self-Ligating Self-ligating braces use a specialized clip instead of elastic bands to hold the wire in place, reducing friction and treatment time. These braces often require fewer visits to the orthodontist and are more comfortable. Invisalign® and Clear Aligners Invisalign clear aligners are a discreet and comfortable alternative to traditional braces, using a series of removable trays to gradually shift teeth into place.

Recovery from orthognathic surgery can take several weeks, with patients typically wearing braces before and after the procedure. Post-surgery care focuses on managing swelling, pain, and maintaining a proper diet. While the recovery period requires patience, the long-term benefits of improved function and appearance are well worth the effort.

We view your Course of Care as an investment in both time and cost, as it is a comprehensive approach to improving both your dental function and appearance. Dr. Horani will guide you every step of the way, ensuring that you receive the most effective and personalized care. Braces Traditional and Twin Brackets Traditional braces are the most common type of orthodontic treatment, using metal brackets and wires to gradually shift teeth into place.

They are virtually invisible, allowing you to straighten your teeth without drawing attention to your treatment. These aligners are custom-made to fit your teeth and are designed to be worn for about 20-22 hours a day. With the flexibility to remove them for eating or brushing, clear aligners offer convenience alongside effective results. Orthognathic Surgery Orthognathic surgery, also known as jaw surgery, is a procedure used to correct severe bite issues and jaw misalignments.

Your Course of Care May Include… Based on your unique needs and best outcome, your Course of Care may involve a combination of treatments, including braces, clear aligners, and, if necessary, orthognathic surgery. Dr. Horani will recommend the optimal treatment plan for you, ensuring that each step is designed to achieve the best possible results for your oral health and facial harmony.

Technology iTero Lumina The iTero Lumina is a state-of-the-art digital scanner that uses advanced light-based technology to create precise, 3D impressions of your teeth without the need for messy molds. It allows for quick, comfortable scans that provide highly accurate data for treatment planning. CBCT Cone Beam Computed Tomography (CBCT) provides 3D imaging of your teeth, jaw, and surrounding structures.

This technology offers a comprehensive view, improving accuracy for complex diagnoses and treatment planning. It is typically recommended when orthodontic treatments alone cannot address the problem. This surgery improves both function and appearance by repositioning the upper and lower jaws. Orthognathic surgery is often necessary for patients with significant jaw discrepancies that affect chewing, speaking, or breathing. It can also help alleviate discomfort from TMJ disorders or sleep apnea caused by jaw misalignment. In many cases, surgery is combined with braces to achieve the best results.

Whether you’re due for a routine check-up or need assistance with your current treatment, we’re here to ensure your experience remains exceptional every step of the way. We understand that life can get busy, so we strive to make the process as convenient as possible. Simply reach out to us to book your next visit, and we’ll find a time that works best for you. com --> Financing & Insurance Currently, we are accepting insurance as out-of-network, and we want to educate our patients that in-network orthodontists aren’t the only providers covered by insurance. Many insurance plans will offer coverage for out-of-network providers, and often the costs are not as high as most expect when choosing providers outside the network.
